timeline.label: Trustees declared $0.05/unit distribution citing lower April royalty and no bonus; July 30 royalty report due.
Q1 2026 royalty report: 938,572 tons shipped, bonus royalty $0 (below $71.70/ton threshold).
Mesabi Trust commenced AAA arbitration vs Northshore/Cliffs on idling and intercompany royalty underpayment.
Cliffs paid $71.185M satisfying prior AAA award (~$5.43/unit); non-recurring distribution.
AAA panel awarded $59.8M damages plus pre-award interest in prior Cliffs royalty dispute.
Northshore idled May 2022–April 2023; subsequent arbitration subject matter for 2025 panel.
Cleveland-Cliffs completed acquisition of remaining Northshore interest; operator concentration increases.
Mesabi Trust formed as passive royalty trust on Northshore Mining pellet leases.

industry.structure: U.S. iron ore pellet royalties are concentrated among a handful of Great Lakes operators; Mesabi Trust is a passive claimant on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore swing mine with no operating control.
industry.share_shift: Cliffs/Northshore controls production, shipments, and bonus-tier pricing inputs; trust unitholders are price-takers on operator decisions and pellet market cycles.
industry.trend: Pellet demand tied to domestic EAF steel; bonus tiers make distributions highly variable with realized pellet prices; legal disputes over intercompany pricing add event risk.
